trade.summary: Bilateral trade is modest. Indonesia exports palm oil, textiles, electronics components, and rubber. South Africa exports minerals (manganese, chrome, platinum group metals), vehicles, chemicals, and agricultural products. Both countries are G20 members seeking to expand South-South trade.

diplomatic.summary: Both are major emerging economies and G20 members. Relations are collegial but not deep. Both share non-aligned traditions and cooperate in multilateral forums. Both are members of IBSA (India, Brazil, South Africa) and BRICS adjacent groupings. South Africa's 2023 BRICS expansion is relevant context.

military.summary: No significant military cooperation. Some engagement through multilateral UN peacekeeping frameworks.

financial.summary: Limited FDI in either direction. Some South African mining companies have explored Indonesian mineral opportunities. Indonesian palm oil investment in South Africa is minimal.

energy.summary: No significant bilateral energy trade. Both are major coal producers but in different markets. Cooperation on just energy transition is a growing area of common interest.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific cooperation. Some exchange through multilateral research networks. Both countries are interested in South-South technology transfer in agriculture and health.
